financial.note: MBK holds ~24.9% of TCAP; likewise, TCAP holds ~23% of MBK. Their performance is deeply intertwined through equity income and shared board representation.
TCAP is a key shareholder (~24.9%+). In early 2026, TCAP sought BoT approval to exceed 25% ownership due to TTB's share buybacks, emphasizing TTB's role as a dividend engine.
Holds ~1.14% stake; represents state-linked institutional stability and alignment with government investment themes.

supply_chain.note: Core profit contributor; 2025/2026 performance driven by non-life insurance growth and bancassurance synergy with TTB.
TCAP holds ~60%. Specializes in truck leasing; earnings are highly sensitive to the Thai logistics sector and industrial NPL trends.
Provides brokerage and investment banking services; performance correlates with SET trading volume and IPO activity.

competitors.note: Direct rival for high-dividend yield bank investors and hire-purchase market share.
Competes in the hire-purchase, wealth management, and securities sectors.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a holding company for leasing and banking assets, rising rates increase the cost of funds for THANI and TTB, potentially squeezing interest spreads.
High debt levels (~90% in 2026) act as a ceiling for hire-purchase loan growth and pose persistent NPL risks for THANI.
Directly impacts the performance of MBK's retail malls and Thanachart Insurance's motor premium volume.
